title: euphorbia deltoidea subsp. serpyllum
intro: Euphorbia deltoidea subsp. serpyllum, also known as spurge or serpent's tongue, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, derived from its latex and leaves. The health benefits associated with Euphorbia deltoidea subsp. serpyllum include its potential use in treating skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's latex and leaves can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new herbal remedies.
